prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(create_route_profile origin_leg_profile_1)
(match_route_profile_to_fleet_type origin_leg_profile_1 fleet_type_light_van)
(assign_time_window_to_route_profile origin_leg_profile_1 time_window_slot_1)
(finalize_route_profile_validation origin_leg_profile_1 time_window_slot_1)
(reserve_cargo_for_origin_leg origin_leg_profile_1 origin_node_a cargo_unit_pallet_1)
(finalize_origin_leg_readiness origin_leg_profile_1 origin_node_a time_window_slot_1 cargo_unit_pallet_1)
(release_time_window_from_route_profile origin_leg_profile_1 time_window_slot_1)
(create_route_profile destination_leg_profile_1)
(match_route_profile_to_fleet_type destination_leg_profile_1 fleet_type_heavy_truck)
(assign_time_window_to_route_profile destination_leg_profile_1 time_window_slot_1)
(finalize_route_profile_validation destination_leg_profile_1 time_window_slot_1)
(reserve_cargo_for_destination_leg destination_leg_profile_1 destination_node_b cargo_unit_pallet_1)
(finalize_destination_leg_readiness destination_leg_profile_1 destination_node_b time_window_slot_1 cargo_unit_pallet_1)
(release_time_window_from_route_profile destination_leg_profile_1 time_window_slot_1)
(assign_time_window_to_route_profile origin_leg_profile_1 time_window_slot_1)
(assemble_transport_leg origin_leg_profile_1 destination_leg_profile_1 origin_node_a destination_node_b transport_leg_1)
(activate_transport_leg_for_loading transport_leg_1 origin_leg_profile_1 time_window_slot_1)
(dispatch_origin_leg origin_leg_profile_1 transport_leg_1)
(dispatch_destination_leg destination_leg_profile_1 transport_leg_1)
(create_route_profile vehicle_unit_1)
(match_route_profile_to_fleet_type vehicle_unit_1 fleet_type_intermodal)
(release_time_window_from_route_profile origin_leg_profile_1 time_window_slot_1)
(assign_time_window_to_route_profile vehicle_unit_1 time_window_slot_1)
(finalize_route_profile_validation vehicle_unit_1 time_window_slot_1)
(register_vehicle_equipment vehicle_unit_1 vehicle_equipment_refrigeration_unit)
(assign_depot_to_route_profile vehicle_unit_1 depot_main)
(assign_container_to_transport_leg vehicle_unit_1 container_unit_1 transport_leg_1)
(prepare_container_assignment_on_vehicle vehicle_unit_1 container_unit_1 transport_leg_1 time_window_slot_1)
(assign_vehicle_availability_constraint vehicle_unit_1 vehicle_availability_constraint_weekend_restriction)
(vehicle_capability_register_stage2_primary vehicle_unit_1 vehicle_equipment_refrigeration_unit depot_main container_unit_1 transport_leg_1)
(vehicle_capability_register_stage3_primary vehicle_unit_1 vehicle_availability_constraint_weekend_restriction container_unit_1 transport_leg_1)
(finalize_vehicle_pretrip vehicle_unit_1)
(attach_route_requirement_tag_to_profile vehicle_unit_1 route_requirement_hazardous time_window_slot_1)
(bind_fleet_type_and_mark_vehicle_assigned vehicle_unit_1 fleet_type_intermodal route_requirement_hazardous)
(create_route_profile vehicle_unit_2)
(match_route_profile_to_fleet_type vehicle_unit_2 fleet_type_intermodal)
(release_time_window_from_route_profile vehicle_unit_1 time_window_slot_1)
(assign_time_window_to_route_profile vehicle_unit_2 time_window_slot_1)
(finalize_route_profile_validation vehicle_unit_2 time_window_slot_1)
(unregister_vehicle_equipment vehicle_unit_1 vehicle_equipment_refrigeration_unit)
(register_vehicle_equipment vehicle_unit_2 vehicle_equipment_refrigeration_unit)
(release_depot_from_route_profile vehicle_unit_1 depot_main)
(assign_depot_to_route_profile vehicle_unit_2 depot_main)
(remove_vehicle_availability_constraint vehicle_unit_1 vehicle_availability_constraint_weekend_restriction)
(assign_vehicle_availability_constraint vehicle_unit_2 vehicle_availability_constraint_weekend_restriction)
(assign_container_to_transport_leg vehicle_unit_2 container_unit_2 transport_leg_1)
(prepare_container_assignment_on_vehicle vehicle_unit_2 container_unit_2 transport_leg_1 time_window_slot_1)
(vehicle_capability_register_stage2_primary vehicle_unit_2 vehicle_equipment_refrigeration_unit depot_main container_unit_2 transport_leg_1)
(vehicle_capability_register_stage3_primary vehicle_unit_2 vehicle_availability_constraint_weekend_restriction container_unit_2 transport_leg_1)
(finalize_vehicle_pretrip vehicle_unit_2)
(attach_route_requirement_tag_to_profile vehicle_unit_2 route_requirement_hazardous time_window_slot_1)
(bind_fleet_type_and_mark_vehicle_assigned vehicle_unit_2 fleet_type_intermodal route_requirement_hazardous)
(release_time_window_from_route_profile vehicle_unit_2 time_window_slot_1)
(assign_time_window_to_route_profile origin_leg_profile_1 time_window_slot_1)
(attach_route_requirement_tag_to_profile origin_leg_profile_1 route_requirement_hazardous time_window_slot_1)
(bind_fleet_type_and_mark_origin_leg_assigned origin_leg_profile_1 fleet_type_light_van route_requirement_hazardous)
(release_time_window_from_route_profile origin_leg_profile_1 time_window_slot_1)
(assign_time_window_to_route_profile destination_leg_profile_1 time_window_slot_1)
(attach_route_requirement_tag_to_profile destination_leg_profile_1 route_requirement_hazardous time_window_slot_1)
(bind_fleet_type_and_mark_destination_leg_assigned destination_leg_profile_1 fleet_type_heavy_truck route_requirement_hazardous)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is a planning problem in PDDL for fleet type matching. The goal is to make (resource_bound origin_leg_profile_1), (resource_bound destination_leg_profile_1), (resource_bound vehicle_unit_1), and (resource_bound vehicle_unit_2).


## Investigating problem scope  
- The task is a planning problem framed in PDDL for fleet type matching.  
- The goal involves ensuring resource bounds for origin_leg_profile_1, destination_leg_profile_1, vehicle_unit_1, and vehicle_unit_2.

## Investigating problem details  
- Origin_leg_profile_1, destination_leg_profile_1, vehicle_unit_1, and vehicle_unit_2 are all classified as route_profiles.  
- These profiles need to undergo actions like creating profiles, but initially, none have profile_created or profile_validated.  

## Examining initialization state  
- The initial state lacks created or validated profiles for the objects involved.  
- Transport_leg_1 is unassigned, affecting the readiness for dispatch of leg profiles.  

## Analyzing resource binding process  
- Binding resources requires profiles to have profile_requirements_attached, which depends on being ready_for_dispatch.  
- For legs, ready_for_dispatch needs the transport_leg to be instantiated and activated for loading.  

## Exploring action dependencies  
- Attaching route requirement tags to profiles requires readiness for dispatch, a time window assignment, and an available tag.  
- Profile assignment to fleet types needs the profile to be created first, adding steps before resource binding.

## Exploring readiness mechanisms  
- Transport legs need to be instantiated and activated for loading to achieve ready_for_dispatch.  
- For vehicles, pretrip or permit actions likely contribute to readiness, requiring additional steps.  

## Investigating tag and fleet constraints  
- Only one route_requirement_tag_available (route_requirement_hazardous) exists, but it can be reused across multiple bindings as actions consume and release it.  
- Fleet types can also be assigned to multiple profiles, with three fleets available, though the goal doesn't specify which fleet to use.  

## Analyzing assembly processes  
- To assemble transport_leg, both legs must be ready_for_assembly and ready, with nodes also prepared.  
- Preparing origin_node_readiness for origin_leg_profile_1 requires validation and a time window assignment, which may need sequencing if shared.  

## Examining alternative approaches  
- Reserve_cargo_for_origin_leg offers a depot and time-free way to achieve readiness, requiring validation and cargo availability.  
- This method sets ready_for_assembly and assigns cargo, potentially simplifying the process for legs.

## Exploring vehicle readiness  
- Vehicles can achieve ready_for_dispatch via a permit path, requiring profile validation and availability constraints like weekend_restriction.  
- Actions like approve_permit_and_mark_vehicle_ready set pretrip_flag and readiness, independent of transport_leg instantiation.  

## Investigating operator qualifications  
- Operator qualifications (cert_a and cert_b) are assigned to vehicle_unit_1 and vehicle_unit_2, respectively, but aren't required for the permit path.  
- The availability of qualifications doesn't impact the readiness process directly, focusing instead on constraints and permits.  

## Analyzing constraint management  
- Weekend_restriction is the only available constraint, assigned and consumed during permit checks, but can be released via remove_vehicle_availability_constraint.  
- This allows sequential assignment to multiple vehicles, ensuring flexibility in the readiness process.  

## Examining leg assembly challenges  
- Assembling transport_leg requires both origin and destination legs to be ready_for_assembly and ready, creating a dependency loop with dispatch.  
- Legs need validation, time assignments, and cargo reservation to achieve readiness before assembly, potentially resolving the chicken-egg issue.

## Checking operator qualifications  
- Vehicle v1 uses operator_qualification_cert_a, consuming it during reserve_permit, with no release action available, leaving cert_a unavailable afterward.  
- Vehicle v2 has operator_qualification_cert_b, but it's not listed as available initially, limiting options for v2's permit process.  

## Exploring vehicle readiness for v2  
- For vehicle v2 to achieve readiness, releasing consumed resources like depot and constraint from v1 is necessary, using actions like release_depot_from_route_profile and remove_vehicle_availability_constraint.  
- However, v2 cannot use cert_a (only available for v1) and lacks access to cert_b, creating a potential bottleneck for resource binding.  

## Analyzing resource constraints  
- Time windows, depots, and constraints can be reassigned after use, but operator qualifications remain consumed without a release mechanism, impacting scalability.  
- The goal requires both vehicles to be resource_bound, but limited operator qualification availability may prevent v2 from proceeding.

## Examining vehicle capability stages  
- The process involves multiple stages (stage1, stage2, stage3) for vehicle capability, starting with vehicle_capability_register_stage2_primary requiring equipment registration and depot assignment.  
- Stage3 (vehicle_capability_register_stage3_primary) needs a constraint assigned and doesn't require special handling, setting the stage for finalizing pretrip.  

## Finalizing vehicle pretrip  
- Finalize_vehicle_pretrip requires stage3 completion and doesn't need service_option_assigned, allowing readiness without additional constraints.  
- This path doesn't require operator qualifications, aligning with resource availability and simplifying the process for both vehicles.  

## Coordinating transport leg and container assignment  
- Assigning containers to transport legs (tl) requires the leg to be instantiated and activated, needing origin_leg ready_for_assembly and timed.  
- Multiple containers (e.g., container1 for v1, container2 for v2) can be assigned to the same tl, ensuring flexibility in vehicle coordination.  

## Managing shared resources  
- The refrigeration_unit is available for equipment registration, and vehicles can register without specific compatibility checks, enhancing flexibility.  
- Constraints can be assigned, used for stage3, and removed afterward, allowing sequential use for multiple vehicles like v1 and v2.

## Releasing and reassignment of resources  
- Unregistering vehicle equipment (e.g., refrigeration_unit) from v1 makes it available for v2, enabling flexible equipment sharing.  
- Depots and constraints assigned to v1 can be released and reassigned to v2, ensuring both vehicles access necessary resources.  

## Managing container and vehicle assignments  
- Container2 is assigned to transport leg 1 for v2 after validating compatibility and availability, locking it for use.  
- Preparing container assignment triggers stage1 for v2, setting the stage for further capability registration.  

## Advancing vehicle capability stages  
- V2 progresses through stage2 and stage3 after equipment registration and constraint assignment, aligning with the process for v1.  
- Finalizing pretrip for v2 sets it as ready_for_dispatch, completing vehicle preparation without needing operator qualifications.  

## Coordinating time window adjustments  
- Releasing the time window from v2 allows reassigning it to origin_leg_profile_1, ensuring timing alignment for leg requirements.  
- Attaching route requirement tags (e.g., hazardous) to origin legs requires ready_for_dispatch and proper time assignment, finalizing resource binding.

## Managing vehicle and fleet assignments  
- Vehicle1 (v1) is bound to fleet_type_intermodal with hazardous requirements, making both intermodal and tags available.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
